name: Code duplication follows community conventions
description: The code duplication ratio is maintained at a reasonable level according to community's standards and conventions. Code duplication ratio is the percentage of code within a codebase or project that is syntactically identical, often meassured using code blocks (i.e. Sonarqube). An example for calculating it could be (lines_duplicated/lines of code)*100.

source.name: Socially-Informed Jupyter Notebook Quality: A Role- and Lifecycle-Aware Metrics Framework
